keyRules: Maximum altitude: 122 m (400 ft) above ground level (AGL)
ALL unmanned aircraft must be registered with the DGAC via the SISAC portal—with no exceptions based on weight or use
The registration number or QR code must be visible on the drone
Always carry your DGAC certificate with you when operating in public spaces
Always keep the drone within direct line of sight (VLOS)
Flight permitted only during the day — night flights require specific authorization from the DGAC
🚫 GALÁPAGOS: Recreational drones are COMPLETELY PROHIBITED throughout the archipelago
Areas near airports and airfields: prior coordination with the DGAC is required
Commercial operation: requires additional certification + overflight permits depending on the area
Do not fly over people not involved in the operation
importantNotes: 📋 RDAC 101 (effective as of December 2024): new UAS regulation approved by the DGAC Standards Committee on October 24, 2024. Applies to ALL individuals (recreational, private, professional) operating drones with a maximum takeoff weight (MTOW) of up to 250 kg. ELIMINATES the previous weight-based exemption under Regulation 2020-0110.

🆔 REGISTRATION IS MANDATORY FOR EVERYONE: Unlike most Latin American countries, Ecuador has NO weight threshold—even a DJI Mini (<250 g) or a toy drone must be registered with SISAC. The system issues a certificate with a unique QR code.

🐢 GALÁPAGOS — STRICT RESTRICTIONS: Recreational drones are PROHIBITED throughout the archipelago. You can bring your drone (it will not be confiscated at customs), but you CANNOT fly it. Operations are permitted only with special authorization for: scientific research, rescue, environmental monitoring, and commercial/educational audiovisual production. Fines and deportation are possible.

✈️ FOREIGN TOURISTS: The rules apply equally to non-residents. Before traveling: Register your drone with SISAC (you may be asked to provide an Ecuadorian ID or passport, along with the drone’s specifications). If you’re visiting the Galápagos, consider leaving your drone on the mainland—you won’t be able to fly it there.

🌳 OTHER NATIONAL PARKS: Cotopaxi, Yasuní, Cajas, Sangay, and other protected areas managed by the Ministry of the Environment require specific permits for drones. Check with the Ministry of the Environment and the relevant park before flying.

primaryLinks.title: DGAC — Drone Registry (SISAC)
primaryLinks.description: Official DGAC website detailing the drone registration process via the SISAC portal. RDAC 101 requires the registration of ALL unmanned aircraft, regardless of weight or use (recreational/commercial).

primaryLinks.title: DGAC Ecuador — Home Page
primaryLinks.description: Official website of the General Directorate of Civil Aviation. Access to RDAC regulations, procedures, certifications, and aeronautical services

primaryLinks.title: DGAC — Introduction to Drone Use
primaryLinks.description: Official DGAC training materials for drone operators: RDAC 101, requirements, procedures, and pilot obligations

primaryLinks.title: Overflight Permits (Gob.ec)
primaryLinks.description: Official procedure via Gob.ec to apply for overflight permits. Required for operations in controlled or restricted areas

secondaryLinks.title: RDAC 101 — UAS Regulations (PDF)
secondaryLinks.description: Official text of the Regulations on Remotely Piloted Aircraft (2020 version, on which RDAC 101—effective as of December 2024—was based). Check with the DGAC for the most recent version.

secondaryLinks.title: Galápagos National Park — Drone Regulations
secondaryLinks.description: Official website of the Galápagos National Park. Recreational drones are PROHIBITED throughout the archipelago—only scientific research, rescue operations, environmental monitoring, and audiovisual production are permitted with a permit.

secondaryLinks.title: Aeronaut Systems — Drone Pilot Courses (DGAC-Authorized)
secondaryLinks.description: Company authorized by the DGAC to offer drone pilot courses. Official training and certification in accordance with RDAC 101

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the DGAC’s official sources before flying. This app is a tool, not a legal authority. Ecuador enforces RDAC 101 (effective as of December 2024)—ALL unmanned aircraft must be registered on the SISAC portal, with no exceptions based on weight. The Galápagos Islands have special restrictions.
